It is a traditional medicinal plant used in Ayurvedic medicine, prized for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptogramma chandrae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
